chunks.title: Introduction to Boron and Carnivore Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses the mineral boron, noting its absence in a strict carnivore diet. They share personal experiences with boron supplementation, citing improvements in sex hormone binding globulin levels.
chunks.transcript: back to the discussion of boron boron is a mineral it's something i first came across when i was doing strict carnivore because what we realize fairly quickly with strict carnivore is that there are no sources of boron on a carnivore diet so when i was a strict carnivore i did actually supplement with boron and saw a significant decline in my section one binding globulin that appeared to be temporary people have talked about perhaps cycling boron if you take boron consistently the shpg appears to go back up but it is interesting that at least acutely or in some ways sex hormone binding globulin responds to

chunks.title: Boron and Hormonal Effects
chunks.summary: The conversation shifts to the role of boron in hormone regulation, discussing its relationship with testosterone and estrogen levels. The speaker references studies that suggest boron influences sex hormone binding globulin.
chunks.transcript: levels of boron boron is probably a nutrient that is vital for humans and questions remain about the adequacy of boron in our soils when i've looked into this there are some maps of boron and soils but it's not totally clear where soils are boron deficient versus boron sufficient but there is good epidemiologic evidence in cohorts of people who live in regions of the world where they are getting more boron versus less boron that there are significant benefits the most widely studied is with regard to arthritis but again boron does appear to have effects on sex from one binding globulin which will affect levels of free testosterone boron does also appear to affect estrogen levels though it's unclear whether those go up or down boron also appears to improve cognition i'll show you studies to support all of those things there's also some suggestion that boron adequacy affects vitamin d levels and that boron supplementation may be valuable for people who are vitamin d non-responders i find that quite interesting apparently boron inhibits an enzyme in the human body known as 24-hydroxylase which is an enzyme that breaks down vitamin d is it possible then that subclinical boron deficiency could result in excess activity of 24 hydroxylase and non-response of vitamin d levels to vitamin d supplementation it's possible i certainly have heard anecdotes of people who say i take vitamin d but my vitamin d doesn't go up and i'm curious about that i wonder what would happen if we put them in the sun or a tanning bed which i think is fine as long as you don't get burned some people have responded that tanning beds don't have infrared light to which i've responded well you can just go outside and get plenty of infrared even in the winter uh by being in the sun so i think most of us are exposed to a variety of visible and invisible wavelengths when we're outside and if we want ultraviolet wavelengths which in some parts of the world are in short supply at certain

chunks.title: Sources of Boron in Diet
chunks.summary: The speaker identifies dietary sources of boron, particularly fruits like avocados, raisins, and plums. They express concerns about the bioavailability of boron in different foods, especially in conjunction with other minerals.
chunks.transcript: latitudes during the winter i think we should use a tanning bed or it's one reasonable strategy on previous podcasts i've spoken about the benefits of getting in sunlight or ultraviolet light relative to just taking vitamin d it's something i've also spoken about on instagram on my content there but with regard to boron is it possible that boron deficiency affects this enzyme leading to non-response to vitamin d supplementation i think it is maybe another reason to supplement with boron so where is boron found boron is found in a variety of foods the highest foods with boron that i have found are things like avocados raisins dates plums prune juice are the fruits that have boron i believe apples have a moderate amount of boron too if you look at listings of boron foods you'll also find boron in things like nuts and seeds but i have some concerns about the bioavailability of that boron and i don't know if that's ever actually been studied one of the things we know clearly is that minerals like zinc which are divalent cations are not very bioavailable in any of those foods so i've spoken about this with regard to magnesium as well though foods like beans or lentils or almonds are said to have magnesium that magnesium is likely very poorly bioavailable the studies we have are with regard to zinc and the fact that if you give someone oysters you see their zinc levels in the blood rise oysters are a great source of bioavailable zinc but if you add black beans or tortillas or both you see a significant attenuation in fact a complete abrogation a complete loss of bioavailability of that zinc when you add foods that are rich in oxalates and phytic acid those molecules are meant to chelate those divalent cations and will cause problems with their absorption yet again i'm not a fan of trying to get your minerals from beans or nuts or grains or seeds for that reason so i have doubts about the bioavailability of boron in those foods though i don't know that it's ever been studied directly in my diet i think what i'm going to do is an experiment including more dates and raisins i'm always trying to get organic and perhaps some dried plums along with some apples if i can find them organically in costa rica to the point that i'm trying to get around three milligrams of boron per day in my foods i think it's totally doable though it does mean that i end up eating a lot of raisins and dates uh dried plums and

chunks.title: Research on Boron and Cognitive Performance
chunks.summary: Research is presented showing a correlation between dietary boron intake and cognitive function. The studies demonstrate that low boron levels are associated with poorer cognitive performance in manual dexterity and memory tasks.
chunks.transcript: a couple of apples a day it's not the worst thing to do but i'm curious how it affects my sex from one binding globulin moving forward the next step of the experiment may be some supplementation with three to six grams of boron in the future and i'll keep you guys posted about those experiments but i want to show you some research with regard to boron and challenge you to think about where you're getting boron in your diet so in this study they're looking at three studies of healthy older men and women they did spectral analysis of eeg electroencephalographic data looking at brain waves they looked the effects of dietary boron in two of the three studies when low boron intake was compared to the high boron intake there was a significant with a p value of less than .05 increase in the proportion of low frequency activity and a decrease in the proportion of higher frequency activity an effect often observed in response to general malnutrition and heavy metal toxicity so they said that higher levels of boron were better and lower levels of boron made the eeg look a little bit like general nutrition and heavy metal toxicity that's not good when compared when contrasted with the high boron intake low dietary boron resulted in significantly poorer performance on tasks emphasizing manual dexterity eye hand coordination attention and coding and short-term memory collectively the data from these three studies indicate that boron may play a role in human brain function and cognitive performance and provide additional evidence that boron is an essential nutrient for humans i found that to be quite interesting i

chunks.title: Boron Deficiency and Its Impacts
chunks.summary: The speaker discusses potential consequences of boron deficiency, connecting it to arthritis and overall health. They mention how boron supplementation could improve health markers related to age-related conditions.
chunks.transcript: don't think there's a lot of discussion around boron and brain performance that i've heard but it made me reconsider where i was getting my boron from and we have to realize that some of the food we get may be grown in boron-deficient soils there was in fact some discussion of this with regard to pineapples which i found germaine as i am in costa rica now i'll show you this article on pineapples you can see here the difference is in the size of the pineapple fruit this is a team that makes borax which is a boron supplement quote unquote for soils but apparently boron deficiency can change the size and shape of pineapples so i'll be looking for boron adequate pineapples in the future there are a couple of small trials like this one on testosterone and sex hormone binding globulin levels in males both of the studies did show an increase in testosterone with one of the studies showing a very significant increase in testosterone and a decrease in estrogen the other study showing a more modest increase in testosterone and actually an increase in estrogen so i think it's unclear exactly what happens with regard to hormones and boron but based on these studies it does look like boron affects hormones in the human body if boron is indeed a nutrient that we need and i think those cognitive studies and the osteoarthritis studies i'm about to show you do suggest that boron is valuable for humans i suspect that getting a reasonable amount of boron is probably a an advantageous thing to do for your hormones and i'm curious how it will affect my section one binding globulin in the long term you can see here in this study they say supplementation with 10 milligrams of boron per day for four weeks uh resulted in 84 of the subtle metal dose being recovered in the urine plasma estradiol concentrations increased significantly as a result of supplementation and there was a trend for plasma testosterone levels to be increased before all the men out there get worried about plasma estradiol increasing know that as a man it's important to have adequate levels of estrodial in your human body so that your lipids are normal and whatnot i don't think levels of estradiol will go up to gynecomastia or pathological levels in males and i think that some of the effects on vitamin d have to do with 17 beta estradiol which is a critical hormone for both men and women and that hormone interacts with 24 hydroxylase so this is another study by the same author the comparative effects of daily and weekly boron supplementation on plasma steroid hormones and pro-inflammatory cytokines should be noted that the effects with regard to pro-inflammatory cytokines appear positive so they say here born in the plasma increased significantly following hours and weekly consumption six hours of supplementation showed a significant decrease on sex hormone binding globulin hscrp tnf alpha after one week in samples taken at 8 am only the mean plasma free testosterone increased the mean plasma estradiol decreased significantly dihydrotestosterone cortisol and vitamin d increased also concentrations of three inflammatory biomarkers decreased after supplementation of note despite decreased pro-inflammatory cytokines based on recent data this must be the first human study to reportedly show an increase in levels of free testosterone after boron consumption unfortunately probably because most studies are funded by pharmaceuticals and the pharmaceutical industry may not make any money on boron i don't think there's been many other studies on boron supplementation in humans if you guys are aware of more and can add it please put it in the youtube comments or dm me on instagram or whatever please share the studies on boron but i do think that this is an important mineral that is often lost if you're doing a strict carnivore diet the best place to get your boron is probably in bones i think boron is in the long bones and that's why when i was a carnivore i would use things like bone meal or bone matrix from hardened soil supplements for boron but now that i have some fruit in my diet that's an easier way to get my boron and i get my calcium from raw milk if you do not tolerate dairy it's another reason to consider something like bone matrix from heart and soil supplements the data with regard to boron and joints is impressive this is a study called the essentiality of boron for healthy bones and joints and they note that these findings included a analytical evidence of lower boron concentrations in femur heads bones and synovial fluid from people with arthritis than from those without this disorder observational evidence that bones of patients using boron supplements are much harder to cut than those of patients not using supplements that's interesting epidemiologic evidence that in areas of the world where boron intake is less than one milligram or less per day the estimated incidence of arthritis ranges from twenty to seventy percent that's a lot of arthritis whereas in areas of the world where boron intakes are three to ten milligrams the estimated incidence of arthritis ranges from zero to ten

chunks.title: Boron Supplementation and Joint Health
chunks.summary: This segment highlights studies showing the benefits of boron supplementation for joint health, particularly in those with osteoarthritis. The potential for improved joint health and bone density is emphasized.
chunks.transcript: percent that's a lot lower it makes me think that i'm gonna give my mom some boron supplements my mom is 72 and she's had osteoarthritis her whole life i think it's probably related to some of the foods she continues to eat that are triggering her immune system but maybe she's not getting enough boron and maybe most of our foods are deficient in boron it's quite possible do we really know what adequacy is for this mineral experimental evidence that rats with induced arthritis benefit from orally or intraperitoneally administered boron there is experimental evidence from a double-blind placebo-controlled uh supplementation trial with 20 adults with osteoarthritis they were supplemented with six milligrams per day and 50 of the subjects receiving the supplement reported improvement in arthritis compared to only 10 receiving the placebo so there's interesting data on boron and joints boron and osteoarthritis maybe even boron and bone density i find it quite intriguing that people who take boron have bones that are harder to cut through during presumably orthopedic procedures it's an observational finding but i want strong bones i'm out there getting crushed by waves on a pretty much daily basis most of you want strong bones and that is going to certainly help you live a strong and agile life so i think my mom will be trying some boron supplements in the future again personally i am trying to get boron in my diet first and i'll keep an eye on the shbg the section one binding globulin for my mom it's probably easier to take three to six milligrams of boron
